title: Coherent dynamics in chaotic spin chains via interference-protected subspaces
authors: Aron Kerschbaumer, Jean-Yves Desaules, Maksym Serbyn
abstract: Generic quantum many-body systems are expected to thermalize, scrambling initial coherence while local observables relax to equilibrium values. Weak ergodicity breaking, often associated with quantum many-body scarring of homogeneous states, provides rare exceptions with long-lived coherence. We introduce a family of local spin-1/2 chains with a structured subspace that hosts a much broader range of nonthermal phenomena, such as scars, chirally propagating quasiparticles or approximate topological edge modes. These nonthermal phenomena happening at high energy densities can be understood via structured subspaces that are protected by destructive interference. We develop a quantitative leakage theory predicting which states retain coherence and suggest ways to improve the stability by inducing fast oscillations in the complement subspace. Our framework connects asymptotic scars, quantum cages, and parent-Hamiltonian constructions, and shows that weak ergodicity breaking in chaotic systems extends well beyond revivals of homogeneous initial states.
summary: This paper introduces a way to find and stabilize non-thermal phenomena like quantum scars and chiral quasiparticles within chaotic spin chains using interference-protected subspaces. By adding a specific perturbation, the authors show that leakage into the chaotic bulk can be suppressed, allowing for persistent coherent dynamics. This work connects several disparate concepts like quantum cages and many-body scars into a single unified theory.
main_problem: Understanding the mechanism behind weak ergodicity breaking and how certain non-thermal states, such as quantum scars and chiral quasiparticles, can maintain coherence and evade thermalization in chaotic many-body systems.
main_result: The authors identify an interference-protected subspace in a family of chaotic spin chains where non-thermal phenomena are stabilized by destructive interference and rapid oscillations, providing a unified framework for scars, quantum cages, and topological edge modes.
method: The study employs a quantitative leakage theory based on a Dyson expansion in the interaction picture, a tight-binding mapping of the protected subspace, and numerical simulations of real-space dynamics and fidelity.
model_or_system: A family of local, chaotic spin-1/2 chains based on an inversion-symmetric version of the kinetically constrained East model, specifically a perturbed Hamiltonian H_PX with a stabilization term g*H_PXP.
key_observables: Real-space dynamics, fidelity revivals, leakage rate, entanglement entropy, local expectation values, and level-spacing statistics.
important_parameters: The stabilization strength g, momentum k, modulation frequency q, and blockade range alpha.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes the existence of a structured tight-binding subspace and uses a first-order Dyson expansion for the leakage theory.
figures_summary: Figure 1 shows wavepacket and scarred state constructions and chiral dynamics; Figure 2 illustrates leakage suppression mechanisms; Figure 4 compares quasiparticle decay at g=0 vs. stability at g=10; Figure 5 shows fidelity revivals; Figure 6 relates to the parent Hamiltonian construction.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the model and the concept of protected subspaces, develops a quantitative leakage theory, demonstrates various non-thermal phenomena (scars, chiral particles, edge modes), provides a stabilization mechanism via a perturbation term, and concludes by connecting these findings to broader frameworks like parent Hamiltonians.
why_interesting: It provides a new theoretical framework for engineering and stabilizing non-thermal, coherent dynamics (like quantum scars and topological modes) within otherwise chaotic many-body environments, which is highly relevant for controlling many-body dynamics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Coherent dynamics under time-evolution of H(g) PX on a chain with L = 24 sites. Schematic construction of (a) coherently propagating wavepacket |χα⟩and (b) scarred state |¯χη⟩. (c) Real-space dynamics for g = 0 of |χα⟩with α = (9, 4, −π/2). The defect propagates coherently to the right while slowly broadening in time, while the vacuum becomes only weakly perturbed. (d) Real-space dynamics for g = 0 for the scarred state |¯χη⟩with η = (2π/L, π/2) shows periodic but decaying revivals. (e) Collision between two wavepackets propagating in opposite directions for g = 10, given by the initial state |χα,β⟩with α = (13, 4, −π/2) and β = (13, 4, π/2). The quasiparticles interact nontrivially...

FIG. 2. Leakage theory for time evolution under H(g) PX. (a) Schematic of interactions on relevant states for leakage the- ory. Inside HTB (blue), the dynamics is exactly TB-like, while leakage originates from V coupling the |Bm⟩states to H⊥ (red), which can cancel by destructive interference between neighboring amplitudes (visualized by fading of dashed lines). The additional HPXP term acts only nontrivially in H⊥and drives rapid oscillations in a small two-level subspace, thereby suppressing the accumulated leaked weight. (b) Average leak- age rate between t = 0 and t = 1 for TB momentum states |kn⟩, obtained numerically (solid) and from the analytical pre- diction (dashed) for L = 20...

FIG. 3. (a) SSH-like edge physics in the dimerized HPX,SSH spin chain with L = 18 and t1 = 0.6,t2 = 1.4. The top row shows the energy ⟨HPX,SSH⟩and variance Var(HPX,SSH) with regard to the eigenstates of ΠTBHPX,SSHΠTB with nonzero eigenvalues. The middle row shows the dynamics of the edge modes (exact form shown in End Matter) under HPX,SSH with g = 0 (left) and g = 10 (right). (b) Dynamics of |χα⟩with α = (21, 6, π/2) defined on L = 20 sites under evolution with Hg Bloch with F = 0.6. The left plot shows the evolution with g = 0 which leads only to initial signatures of the Bloch oscil- lations until leakage becomes too large, while the right plots with g = 10 shows clean and persistent...

FIG. 5. Scarred dynamics under time evolution with H(g) PX on a chain with L = 24 sites. (a) Fidelity, F(t) = |⟨ψ(0)|ψ(t)⟩|, for g = 0 and different initial states. The scarred state |¯χη1⟩, with η1 = (2π/L, π/2), is built from momentum states close to π/2 and shows pronounced revivals. The state |¯χη2⟩, with η2 = (6π/L, π/2), oscillates faster but with smaller fidelity peaks. In contrast, a random product state |ψr⟩rapidly ther- malizes. Dashed lines show the weight remaining in HTB, namely 1 −p(t) as defined in Eq. (8). (b) Fidelity dynamics for g = 10 for the same scarred states as in (a), showing al- most perfect revivals.

FIG. 4. Dynamics of quasiparticles under time evolution with H(g) PX. (Top) Evolution without suppression (g = 0). In the left panel, the initial state |χα,β⟩is the same as in Fig. 1(e), but the quasiparticles rapidly decay after the colli- sion without leakage suppression. In the right panel, the state |χα⟩with α = (17, 4, −π/4) is centered around momentum −π/4 and quickly spreads incoherently. (Bottom) Evolution with two quasiparticles under leakage suppression (g = 10). The left panel shows a collision between quasiparticles of dif- ferent sizes, with α = (13, 4, −π/2) and β = (35, 8, π/2), displaying nontrivial interaction and approximate recovery of their shape with a phase shift after...
